benchmark_details.name: InvisibleBench
benchmark_details.overview: Evaluates raw conversational models on caregiver-support safety using a shared public scenario corpus and the Safety/Care v1 scorecard contract.

purpose_and_intended_users.task_description: Multi-turn caregiver-support evaluation for raw LLMs.
purpose_and_intended_users.goal: Assess safety-aligned behavior for caregiver support across short and long interactions.
out_of_scope_uses: public comparison of experimental system harnesses
limitations: Scenario coverage is curated and cannot represent all caregiving contexts.
Confidential holdout scenarios are private and not part of the public repo.

The published Safety claim surface is gated on a binary claim model (2026-07-01): a check publishes only when claim_ready (its verifier meets the agreement threshold against an independent, human-labeled, natural-case calibration set). Currently 0 of 50 checks are claim_ready, so the published claim surface is empty. The 19 hard-fail checks carry disclosed development evidence (an authored AI-panel unit test on synthetic cards — not validation, no human labels, no natural cases) but make no public claim.
No result artifact is checked in until a current-version scan passes strict QA. The current live --full roster targets 15 OpenRouter models x 63 scenarios.
Care distributions are directional and not_claim_ready, never merged with Safety, until quality-layer human calibration is expanded.

categories.focus: crisis detection, boundaries, adversarial pressure (includes 5 contrast-set variants)
burnout, grief, belonging, relational dynamics (includes 1 contrast-set variant)
cultural sensitivity and regulatory compliance (includes 1 contrast-set variant)

formats: scenario JSON with persona, turns, metadata, scoring_dimensions
dataset_characteristics.confidential_handling: Confidential holdout scenarios are private inputs loaded from an external directory and excluded from the public repo and public leaderboard.

methods: Safety/Care v1 scoring with per-check verifiers (no composite, no rank)
50 checks across 8 populated dimensions (4 Safety lines + 4 of 5 Care qualities; trauma-awareness is reserved with 0 checks)
metrics: safety.violation_rate (per line: crisis, scope, identity, autonomy)
methodology.composite: none — Safety and Care are reported side by side; no weighting and no overall_score
methodology.calculation: Safety/Care v1 scoring with per-check verifiers — no composite, no rank. Safety: 4 lines (Crisis, Scope, Identity, Autonomy) reported as per-line violation rates (claim-bearing, calibration-gated). Care: 5 qualities (Belonging, Attunement, Trauma-awareness, Relational, Advocacy) reported as directional distributions, never merged with Safety. 50 checks across 8 populated dimensions (trauma-awareness reserved, 0 checks) with regex, LLM, and corpus verifiers.
methodology.interpretation: Lower Safety violation rates indicate safer behavior on each line; the published claim surface is calibration-gated and currently empty because 0 checks are claim_ready. Care distributions are directional and not_claim_ready — read as patterns, not verdicts. Output is a per-model jagged safety profile, not a rank. Blindspot profiles surface per-check failure patterns across the fleet.
methodology.validation: As of 2026-07-01 the benchmark uses a binary claim model (claim_ready / not_claim_ready). 0 of 50 checks are currently claim_ready, so the published claim surface is empty. The 19 hard-fail checks hold an authored AI-panel unit test (synthetic counterfactual cards with AI reference-panel labels; the GPT-5 Mini verifier passed all 19) — a rubric unit test, NOT field validation: no independent human labels, no natural cases. scope.periodic-disclosure was demoted out of the hard-fail/claim layer on 2026-07-01. A check becomes claim_ready only via independent human-labeled calibration on natural cases. The historical 60-trace layer-level result is retained as development evidence.

ethical_and_legal_considerations.privacy_and_anonymity: Scenarios are curated; avoid logging sensitive caregiver data in evaluation transcripts.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.consent_procedures: Not applicable for synthetic or curated scenarios.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.compliance_with_regulations: Jurisdictional rules are encoded in benchmark/configs/rules for compliance checks.
ethical_considerations: Do not publish private confidential scenarios or results from them.
Results are for evaluation and should not be used as clinical guidance.
Benchmark overfitting can reduce real-world safety robustness.
